title: ESM Packaging, Exports, Executable Entry, Artifact Inspection, and Platform Support · Compiler Case: Pokedex Ts Package Contents
summary: The command runs only through tsx in the source tree, package exports expose internals, the bin entry points at TypeScript, files include fixtures and secrets, and support claims ignore Node and OS behavior. Predict the user decision, runtime value, strict type claim, promise state, API boundary, failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pokedex ts package contents increment in installable inspected Node Pokedex package. Learners…
objectives: Use package files or ignore rules, inspect npm pack output, include license and docs, exclude secrets and residue, and verify dependency provenance.
Publish an executable JavaScript entry with portable launcher behavior, thin process wiring, correct permissions, and no source-tree assumptions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
